sentence_reviews.text: Coordination, communication, consistency, scheduling, and fault tolerance enable large-scale training and serving.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Consistency models survey abstract; Fault-tolerance in Distributed Optimization and Machine Learning abstract; AIMA contents.html lines 459-477 and 768-772
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support consistency, replication, scalability, availability, fault tolerance, communication issues, and distributed machine learning/optimization. Scheduling and serving are not directly covered by the inspected passages, so the definition is synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Distributed systems & networking is to connect Algorithms & data structures, Complexity & computability to downstream capabilities including Multimodal, simulated & streaming data, Federated & privacy-preserving learning, Routing, conditional computation & mixture-of-experts.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html lines 75-79 and 768-772; Security, Fault Tolerance, and Communication Complexity in Distributed Systems abstract; Fault-tolerance in Distributed Optimization and Machine Learning abstract
sentence_reviews.notes: The evidence supports algorithms, complexity, communication complexity, fault tolerance, and distributed machine learning. It does not directly support the specific downstream atlas capabilities listed.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Multimodal, simulated & streaming data
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Consistency models survey abstract mentioning big data challenges, data access, scalability, and availability
sentence_reviews.notes: Distributed systems evidence supports scalability and big-data access, but the inspected evidence does not specifically address multimodal, simulated, or streaming data.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Security, Fault Tolerance, and Communication Complexity in Distributed Systems abstract; Fault-tolerance in Distributed Optimization and Machine Learning abstract
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support private inputs distributed among processors, correctness/privacy despite faults, and distributed machine learning. They do not use the exact term federated learning in the inspected passages, so this remains synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Routing, conditional computation & mixture-of-experts
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Consistency models survey abstract mentioning distributed systems, scalability, availability, and latency
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected distributed-systems evidence does not directly support routing, conditional computation, or mixture-of-experts as downstream capabilities.

sentence_reviews.text: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Algorithms & data structures, Complexity & computability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html lines 75-79 and 768-772; Security, Fault Tolerance, and Communication Complexity in Distributed Systems abstract; Consistency models survey abstract
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support algorithmic and complexity dependencies and the existence of formal/mathematical distributed-system models. They do not directly state the empirical-mismatch warning.

sentence_reviews.text: Distributed systems & networking rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Algorithms & data structures.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html lines 75-79; Fault-tolerance in Distributed Optimization and Machine Learning abstract
sentence_reviews.notes: Distributed optimization and fault-tolerant distributed algorithms support algorithms as a prerequisite. The exact rationale wording is graph synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Distributed systems & networking rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Complexity & computability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html lines 768-772; Security, Fault Tolerance, and Communication Complexity in Distributed Systems abstract
sentence_reviews.notes: Communication complexity and computational complexity are directly relevant to distributed-system protocols. Computability itself is not directly supported by the inspected evidence.

edge_reviews.notes: Distributed-system protocols and distributed optimization rely on algorithms and data structures. Core strength is defensible.
Communication and computational complexity are relevant, but the packet evidence does not support computability as strongly as complexity. A narrower dependency label would be easier to defend.
